title: sorbus aria subsp. persica
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. persica, also known as the Whitebeam or Rowan, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. The medicinal properties of Sorbus aria subsp. persica lie in its bark, leaves, and berries, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help to boost the immune system and protect against oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Sorbus aria subsp. persica can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
